The transcribed interviews are available for use by researThe Seminole Oral History Collection is within the Native Americans Oral History Collections. Other Native Americans Oral History Collections also include: The 'Native Americans Oral History Collections' is part of the Samuel Proctor Oral History Program, an affiliated program of the University of Florida's Department of History. Its collections include approximately 4,000 interviews and more than 85,000 pages of transcribed material, making it the largest oral history archive in the South and one of the major collections in the country. The transcribed interviews are available for use by researchers, scholars, students, and anyone interested in exploring the rich cultural heritage and experiences of Native American communities through their own words. The Samuel Proctor Oral History Program is dedicated to preserving the voices of individuals who have shaped or been influenced by historical events and social movements. By capturing these personal narratives, the program aims to provide a comprehensive understanding of the diverse perspectives that contribute to our collective history. The collections cover a wide range of topics including but not limited to Native American life, culture, traditions, struggles for rights, environmental issues, and contributions to society. Researchers can access these valuable resources by visiting the University of Florida's digital archives or contacting the Samuel Proctor Oral History Program directly. Utilizing this wealth of information not only enriches academic research but also fosters a deeper appreciation for the resilience and diversity of Native American communities throughout history. texts.full_text: Republic of Korea, at the college of Veterinary Medicine, Gyeongsang National University, Republic of Korea have examined a stem cell population from third molars called human dental papilla stem cells (DpaSCs). DpaSCs can form dentin and dental pulp, but they also have biological features that are similar to those of bone marrow-derived mesenchymal stem cells (MSCs). mSCs have been very heavily studied. While these stem cells hAve remarkable therapeutic capabilities, they have the disadvantage of only being able to grow in culture or a short period of time. After growing in culture for about a week, MSCs tend to go to sleep and not grow anymore. DPaSCs, however, have a remarkable capacity to grow in culture. Data from work done in the laboratory of Gyu-Jin Ryo has shown they can grow for a longer period of time than MSCs in culture without going to sleep. Therefore, they not only can form a greater number of progeny, but they can also, potentially, form larger tissues and structures. Abstract This document defines two new extensions to the Sieve email filtering language, which are intended to improve the handling of email rejections. The "Reject" extension allows a Sieve script to reject an email message and provide a reason for the rejection. The "Extended Reject" extension further extends this capability by allowing a Sieve script to provide a detailed explanation for the rejection, which can be used to communicate more specific information to the sender. 1. Introduction Sieve is a language for filtering email, as specified in [RFC5228]. It is used by mail transfer agents to process incoming email messages according to a set of user-defined rules. This document introduces two new extensions to Sieve that enhance its ability to reject messages and provide feedbAck to senders. 2. Terminology The key words "MUST", "MUST NOT", "REQUIRED", "SHALL", "SHALL NOT", "SHOULD", "SHOULD NOT", "RECOMMENDED", "MAY", and "OPTIONAL" in this document are to be interpreted as described in [RFC2119]. 3. texts.full_text: Research SCIENCE AND EXPERIENCE OTTFSSENT The fact that experience filters perceptions is the single biggest problem for sociological research. In sociological research, the filtering occurs in four stages: o First, the real-life experiences and passions of sociologists motivate much research. o Second, our values lead us to formulate and adopt favoured theories for interpreting and explaining those problems. o Third, sociologists interpretations are influenced by previous research. o And fourth, the methods we use to gather data mould our perceptions. We can never perceive society in a pure or objective form. What we can do is use techniques of data collection that minimize bias. We can also clearly and publicly describe the filters that influence our perceptions. Doing so: o Enables us to eliminate obvious sources of bias. o Helps others see biases we miss and try to correct for them. Researchers generally try to be objective in order to perceive reality as clearly as possible. Although objectivity is a reality check, subjectivity leads us to define which aspects of reality are worth checking on in the first place. Scientific versus Non-scientific Thinking Scientists, including sociologists, develop ways of collecting, observing, and thinking about evidence that minimize their chance of drawing biased conclusions. 10 types of non-scientific thinking: www.notesolution.com o Knowledge based on tradition science is required to separate valid from invalid knowledge. o Knowledge based on authority scientists should always question authority to arrive at more valid knowledge. o Knowledge based on casual observation uncertainty can be reduced by observing in a conscious and deliberate manner and by recording observations. o Knowledge based on overgeneralization scientists sample cases that

texts.full_text: and virulence profiles, contributing to the varying severity of HFMD outbreaks across the region. The clinical significance of EV71 lies in its potential to cause serious and sometimes fatal complications, particularly in young children. The virus primarily affects infants and young children, with symptoms ranging from mild to severe. In severe cases, EV71 can lead to neurological complications such as encephalitis, meningitis, and acute flaccid paralysis. The high case-fatality rates associated with these complications underscore the importance of understanding the evolution and spread of the virus. From a public health perspective, the increasing epidemic activity of EV71 poses a significant challenge. The rapid spread of the virus and its association with severe neurological sequelae necessitate robust surveillance and response mechanisms. Public health agencies need to implement comprehensive prevention and control strategies, including early detection, vaccination, and public education campaigns, to mitigate the impact of EV71 outbreaks. The evolution of enterovirus 71 underscores the dynamic nature of viral pathogens and the constant battle between viral adaptation and human efforts to control disease outbreaks. Continued research into the genetic, epidemiological, and clinical aspects of EV71 is essential to develop effective intervention strategies and reduce the burden of HFMD associated with this virus. These results indicate that conventional physiotherapy with percussion and postural drainage can be an effective intervention for patients with fibrosis, potentially slowing down the progression of lung function decline compared to using the forced expiratory technique alone. The study highlights the importance of incorporating both techniques into a comprehensive treatment plan for individuals with cystic fibrosis. Postural drainage helps to mobilize mucus and secretions from the airways, while percussion assists in breaking up the mucus and facilitating its removal through coughing. These methods can help iMprove respiratory function and reduce the frequency of pulmonary exacerbations. However, it is crucial for healthcare providers to consider individual patient needs and preferences when recommending physiotherapy protocols. Some patients may find conventional physiotherapy too physically demanding or may have difficulty adhering to a daily routine due to their condition. In such cases, alternative approaches like manual therapy, breathing exercises, or the use of mechanical aids might be considered. Furthermore, the study underscores the significance of long-term follow-up and continuous assessment of treatment efficacy in patients with cystic fibrosis. texts.full_text: code bellow, Counter is a static inner class but it can be instantiated as if it was not: An inner class (whether it be static or not) can be instantiated, just like a normal class can. Making a class static only allows you to access it without creating instance of the enclosing class. Like you did in that code: Counter counter1 = new Task.Counter(); Here you are creating an instance of Counter. But since Counter is a nested class (that is what we call a static inner class), we have to access it like that. Task.Counter is the fully qualified name of Counter class (Add package there). In fact, since your main method is in the Task class only, you can directly use: Counter counter1 = new Counter(); Now, for an inner class (non-static), you can't access that class without any instance of the enclosing class. So to create an instance of Counter, you first need an instance of Task, like this: Task task = new Task(); Counter counter = task.new Counter(); You can combine those statements into one like this: Counter counter = new Task().new Counter(); In simple words, the difference is that

texts.full_text: future, here are some tips: Q: How to find a Word file if it isn't in auto recovery? A: In this case, since you did not mention any specific backup strategies employed prior to the unexpected quit and subsequent loss of unsaved work, recovering your dissertation might prove challenging. Here's what can be done at this point: 1. Recent File Recovery: Word for Mac typically creates temporary files while a document is open; these may still exist on your system even after an abrupt closure. Search for '.tmp' or 'Temp' file extensions in the folder where you last saved, which could contain recoverable data from unsaved changes made during that session. 2. Time Machine Backup: If Time Machine was enabled and backing up while working on your dissertation, it's likely there are backup versions of this document stored within its history. To access these: Go to Finder > Applications; right-click (or Ctrl+Click) Microsoft Word icon then select "Show Package Contents"; navigate through the following path - Content/Resources/; here you'll find a file named 'Word' or something similar depending on your setup, with no extension attached yet still openable via double clicking. 3. File Search: Utilize MacOS's native search function to locate files modified within an hour before Word crashed; this might uncover hidden versions saved automatically by the system that were not picked up in Auto Recovery: Open Spotlight (Command+Space) > type "Kind: Pages" without quotes and replace 'Pages' with other word processing apps you use if necessary. 4. Third-Party Data Recovery Software: There are various data recovery applications designed specifically for macOS, which can scan your hard drive to recover deleted or lost files under certain conditions; however their effectiveness depends on several factors including file types involved as well as whether new content has overwritten any potential space holding remnants of old documents since then. texts.full_text: he air assist fuel injector. The conventional fuel injector and the rail are configured such that a metered quantity of fuel is delivered from the fuel injector to the air assist fuel injector. Additionally, the rail includes a number of passageways that deliver pressurized air to the air assist fuel injector. The air assist fuel injector atomizes the low pressure fuel with the pressurized air and conveys the air and fuel mixture to the combustion chamber of an engine. The pressurized gas and liquid fuel typically travel through the interior of a hollow poppet of conventional air assist fuel injectors, and exit the poppet through slots near a head of the poppet. The poppet is reciprocatable in a leg and a head of the poppet typically lifts off a seat to deliver a plume of atomized fuel from the air assist fuel injector to the combustion chamber of an engine. The seat and leg of such conventional air assist fuel injectors each include a bearing surface to guide movement of the poppet. Unfortunately, the finish dimensions of these bearing surfaces are typically set during complex and time consuming grinding operations. If these finish dimensions are not precise, the air assist fuel injector may fail or function improperly because of improper align

The size analysis was performed using gel filtration chromatography on Sephadex G-150 in the presence of digitonin (0·5 mg/ml). For each receptor subtype studied, two peaks were observed with similar molecular weights which indicated that these receptors are present either alone or associated to other proteins and/or lipids. The percentage distribution for 57000 daltons was about equal between cerebral cortex and striatum while the values of larger sizes in both subtypes (145-152 thousand Dalton) appeared higher than those from cerebrum, suggesting a possible difference at receptor level among different brain regions. This study provides important insights into understanding serotonin receptors distribution across various bovine brains areas which could potentially be extrapolated to other mammals including humans. It also highlights the importance of considering regional differences when studying neurotransmitter systems and their roles in normal physiology as well as pathophysiological conditions such as depression, anxiety disorders or schizophrenia where alterations at serotonin receptor levels are often implicated.
